channel.title: Slop Cannons
channel.description: Rethinking Organizations for the Age of AI. Most engineering orgs are built to explain work, not to do it. A blueprint for putting the people who understand the problem in the builder's seat.
channel.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: SLOP CANNONS: Rethinking Organizations for the Age of AI
item.description: Most engineering orgs are built to explain work, not to do it. A blueprint for putting the people who understand the problem in the builder's seat.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: The Manifesto — Slop Cannons
item.description: The core thesis: most engineering organizations are optimized for legibility to leadership, not value to users. AI exposes this. The compressed org is the answer.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: The Fire Team Model — Slop Cannons
item.description: Pairs of Expert Scalers and Slop Cannons as the atomic unit of the compressed org. Elastic, deployed to the highest-value problem, recomposed weekly.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: Expert Scaler — Slop Cannons
item.description: The deep-system thinker who sees architecture at scale and identifies what doesn't need to be built at all.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: Slop Cannon — Slop Cannons
item.description: The high-velocity builder. Not necessarily an engineer by trade. Ships creative, working MVPs fast. AI tools meet them where they are.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: Domain Experts as Builders — Slop Cannons
item.description: The biggest unlock in applied AI: domain experts from sales, finance, and operations becoming builders, not advisors.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: Legibility vs Value — Slop Cannons
item.description: The central diagnosis: engineering organizations optimized for being intelligible to leadership before being useful to users.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: The Compressed Organization — Slop Cannons
item.description: Not downsizing. Resizing. An org that composes and decomposes around actual problems and makes work legible through systems, not layers.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: AI as Forcing Function — Slop Cannons
item.description: AI doesn't create new organizational problems. It makes existing ones impossible to ignore. When building becomes cheap, coordination overhead becomes visible.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: Engineering Leadership — Slop Cannons
item.description: When the cost of building drops, leadership becomes taste, mentorship, and problem curation. Not people management. Not ceremony.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: Real vs Inherited Complexity — Slop Cannons
item.description: The diagnostic filter: does this process increase the org's ability to create value, or has it become overhead defending its right to exist?
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: Applied AI Value — Slop Cannons
item.description: Getting real business value from AI requires changing tools and org design simultaneously. Change only one and you get faster production of things nobody asked for.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill

item.title: Glossary — Slop Cannons
item.description: Key terms and definitions from Slop Cannons: fire teams, Expert Scalers, Slop Cannons, the compression, legibility vs value, and more.
item.dc:creator: Jason Neill
